metadata.label: Pipeline Automation
import_order: 1. Import all items with issue_type = Epic (parent = WSA-2656)
2. Import all items with issue_type = Story (parent = their Epic summary)
3. Import all items with issue_type = Sub-task (parent = their Story summary)
4. Import Action Items -- root-level items link to WSA-2657, outcome-level items link to their parent Epic

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete
work_items.description: Establish SSH/RDP read-only access, document NGINX deployment process, document AWS account structure and VPC topology, SOC 2 compliance requirements, data classification, Dynamo access governance, and network security controls.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-01.1] Environment Access and Network Discovery
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Obtain SSH/RDP read-only access to NGINX target servers
Document AWS account structure and VPC topology
Map network connectivity paths between GitHub runners and target environments
Identify firewall rules and security groups that affect pipeline connectivity
Document current DNS and load balancer configuration for NGINX
[PA-OC-01.2] Deployment Process Documentation
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Conduct deployment walkthrough with Rex (NGINX build process)
Document all scripts, files, and playbooks Ansible pulls for NGINX build
Map the share drive file locations used in current process
Document manual steps, sequencing, and validation checks Rex performs
Identify any MFA or human-interaction requirements in the current Ansible workflow
[PA-OC-01.3] Security and Compliance Baseline
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Document SOC 2 compliance requirements applicable to pipeline automation
Identify secrets in current config files (certificates, IPs, credentials)
Assess data classification requirements for config artifacts
Document current access control patterns (who has access to what)
Identify shared credentials and hardcoded secrets (e.g., database scripts using shared user accounts)
[PA-OC-01.4] Data Classification for Pipeline Artifacts
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Enumerate data types in NGINX configs: host IPs, certificates, environment variables, customer-specific settings
Determine if any config data contains customer PII, PHI, or regulated data
Classify each data type per DMSi's sensitivity levels (or define levels if none exist)
Document which classified data types will be stored in GitHub repositories
Produce data classification report -- reviewed by GRC before PA-D-02 is resolved
SOC 2 CC6.1 and ISO 27001 A.5.12 require data classification before third-party transmission
[PA-OC-01.5] Dynamo Access Governance Review
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Review MSA for security provisions, NDA terms, and data handling obligations
Define Dynamo's maximum access scope in GitHub repos and deployment infrastructure
Require named individuals for Dynamo admin accounts (no shared accounts)
Define access expiration date or periodic review schedule (recommend quarterly)
Enable audit logging of all Dynamo administrative actions in GitHub
Document access handoff and deprovisioning plan for engagement end
SOC 2 CC6.2 and ISO 27001 A.5.19 require third-party access controls
[PA-OC-01.6] Network Security Documentation
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

work_items.summary: Request TierPoint's network architecture documentation for DMSi's environment
Identify firewall rules required for GitHub runner to NGINX server communication
Confirm TLS version and cipher suites for runner-to-server communication
Determine if outbound traffic inspection (proxy, DLP, IDS) will affect runner connectivity
Document network segmentation boundaries affecting runner placement
ISO 27001 A.8.20 requires documented network security controls
[PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete
work_items.description: Inventory scoped to NGINX pipeline configs required to stand up and run this pipeline. Catalog all NGINX config files, map dependencies, identify environment-specific variations, discover and manage all credentials.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-02.1] NGINX Configuration Inventory
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

work_items.summary: Catalog all NGINX config files and templates used by Ansible
Document config file locations on the share drive
Map config dependencies (which configs reference which other files)
Identify environment-specific config variations (per-client, per-site)
Document current config change frequency and change requesters
Scan all config files for embedded secrets (credentials, certificates, API keys) before any version control migration
[PA-OC-02.2] Broader Configuration Landscape Assessment
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

work_items.summary: Catalog configuration categories: customer runtime, platform, application
Map coverage to customer runtime configs (daily, production, provider settings)
Document app server configurations
Document infrastructure-layer configurations
Capture third-party integration configs
Document current state per config type with risk methodology
[PA-OC-02.3] Credential and Secret Management Plan
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

work_items.summary: Inventory all credentials discovered during config inventory: database accounts, API keys, certificates, service accounts
Define secret management approach per credential type (GitHub Secrets, vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
Define rotation schedule for each credential type (recommend 90-day maximum)
Restrict credential scopes to minimum required permissions per integration
Document credential ownership -- who can create, rotate, and revoke each secret
Enable GitHub secret scanning on all repositories
SOC 2 CC6.1 and ISO 27001 A.5.17 require secret management controls
[PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined
work_items.description: Document export methods, design repository structure, evaluate and POC GitHub runner infrastructure with connectivity to target servers, and confirm Ansible automation feasibility.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-03.1] Export Method and Format Decisions
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

work_items.summary: Document export method for each NGINX config type (file copy, API, CLI)
Select canonical format per config type (YAML, JSON, raw NGINX conf)
Define secret extraction and replacement strategy (placeholders, vault refs)
Validate export methods produce identical output to current process
[PA-OC-03.2] Repository Structure Design
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

work_items.summary: Define repository structure (by config type, client, environment, or hybrid)
Establish branching strategy compatible with automation workflow
Define access patterns: who has read, write, and approve permissions
Document storage and access patterns for SOC 2 compliance
[PA-OC-03.3] GitHub Runner Strategy and POC
work_items.parent: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

work_items.summary: Evaluate AWS account capabilities for hosting GitHub runners
Assess tools VPC vs. per-region runner deployment
Test network connectivity from runner to target NGINX servers
Document runner security posture (IAM roles, network policies)
Validate that Ansible can be executed from the runner (no MFA, no manual interaction)
[PA-OC-04] Configurations in Version Control
work_items.description: All NGINX operational configurations migrated from share drive to GitHub as single source of truth. Every config change is a commit with context. Config ownership mapped and enforced through CODEOWNERS.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-04.1] Config Migration Execution
Export all NGINX configs from share drive using documented methods
Strip secrets and replace with vault references or placeholders
Commit configs to repository with descriptive commit messages
Validate migration completeness (diff against source)
Submit config migration as a formal change request through DMSi's CAB process (include rollback procedure and risk assessment; obtain CAB approval before production configs are migrated)
[PA-OC-04.2] Ownership and Access Control
Define CODEOWNERS file mapping config paths to approvers
Configure branch protection rules
Document the approval workflow for config changes
[PA-OC-05] Build Pipeline Operational
work_items.description: GitHub Actions workflow that generates and validates NGINX configs. Triggered by PR merge. Runs linting, schema validation, and templating to produce deployment-ready artifacts.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-05.1] Build Pipeline Implementation
Create GitHub Actions workflow for NGINX config build
Implement config linting and schema validation steps
Implement config templating with secret injection from vault
Build artifact packaging and versioning
[PA-OC-05.2] Build Validation Suite
Test against multiple environment/client config variations
Validate build output matches expected config structure
Document build pipeline usage for team onboarding
[PA-OC-06] Deploy Pipeline Operational
work_items.description: Automated deployment of validated NGINX configs to target environments using Ansible, triggered by successful build pipeline completion. Includes pre-deploy validation, deployment execution, and post-deploy health checks.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-06.1] Deploy Pipeline Implementation
Configure GitHub Actions to trigger Ansible deployment via runner
Implement pre-deploy validation checks
Implement deployment execution with status reporting at each checkpoint
Build post-deploy health checks and rollback triggers
Test deployment to non-production environment first
[PA-OC-06.2] Rollback and Recovery
Build automated rollback from build artifacts
Test rollback end-to-end in non-production
Document rollback procedures for team
[PA-OC-07] Drift Detection Operational
work_items.description: Background pipeline that periodically compares the GitHub source-of-truth against live server configs and alerts on any divergence. Provides metrics for operational scorecard and prevents unauthorized manual changes.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-07.1] Drift Detection Pipeline Implementation
Build scheduled GitHub Action for drift comparison
Implement config fetch from live servers via runner
Implement diff logic with normalized comparison (ignore whitespace, comments, ordering where appropriate)
Define drift detection cadence (recommend: hourly for critical configs, daily for others)
[PA-OC-07.2] Drift Alerting and Escalation
Integrate drift alerts with PagerDuty or Slack (per PA-D-09)
Define drift severity thresholds: critical (security-relevant configs), warning (operational configs), info (cosmetic)
Define escalation paths per severity level
Build drift detection dashboard for visibility
[PA-OC-08] Pipeline Reliability Validated
work_items.description: All three NGINX pipelines (build, deploy, drift detection) must demonstrate 90%+ success rate for routine deployments before the team advances to enforcement. This gate protects against premature Config-as-Code enforcement on unreliable pipelines.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-08.1] Pipeline Reliability Metrics
Define success/failure criteria for each pipeline type
Implement pipeline run logging and metric collection
Track success rate over 2-week rolling window
[PA-OC-08.2] Error Handling and Circuit Breakers
Implement circuit breakers for partial deploy failures
Build automated rollback from build artifacts
Handle network failures, timeout scenarios, and partial state
[PA-OC-08.3] Pipeline Status Dashboard
Create pipeline status dashboard (approved, deploying, complete, failed)
Load test pipelines across multiple environments
Track mean time to deploy and mean time to rollback
[PA-OC-08.4] Reliability Validation Report
Compile 2-week reliability metrics for all three pipelines
Document any failures, root causes, and fixes applied
Produce sign-off document for Andy and DMSi engineering
[PA-OC-09] Config-as-Code Enforced
work_items.description: The config pipeline becomes the enforced default. No manual NGINX config changes allowed outside the pipeline for covered config types. Drift detection actively flags unauthorized changes. Team trained and following config-as-code workflow.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-09.1] Enforcement and Drift Alerting
Enable config-as-code enforcement for NGINX critical configs
Configure drift detection to alert on unauthorized manual changes
Define exception process for emergency manual changes (with post-hoc pipeline reconciliation)
[PA-OC-09.2] Team Training and Adoption
Conduct training sessions with DMSi engineering on config-as-code workflow
Document runbooks for common config change scenarios (new client, config update, rollback)
Provide hands-on practice in non-production environment
[PA-OC-09.3] Incident Response Plan Update
Update DMSi's incident response plan to reference GitHub Actions pipelines, drift detection alerting, and rollback procedures
Include evidence collection procedures using pipeline logs
SOC 2 CC7.3 and ISO 27001 A.5.26 require current IRP documentation
[PA-OC-09.4] Enforcement Validation Report
Track 4-week enforcement period metrics
Document any manual change exceptions with justification
Produce M5 evidence package for Config-as-Code Enforced milestone
[PA-OC-10] Pipeline Fallback Activated
work_items.description: This outcome activates if PA-OC-08 (Pipeline Reliability) validation fails after maximum iterations. Planned fallback path, not an unplanned failure response.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-10.1] Reliability Failure Root Cause Analysis
Document root cause of reliability failure across all three pipelines
Identify specific failure modes and contributing factors
Assess whether failures are systemic or isolated
[PA-OC-10.2] Revised Implementation Plan
Present options to Cameron/Hilltop: extend timeline, reduce scope, or redesign pipeline architecture
Confirm manual deployment processes remain operational and documented
Document revised plan with updated risk assessment
[PA-OC-11] Pipeline Pattern Replication
work_items.description: Apply the proven pipeline framework (GitHub Actions workflows, runner strategy, drift detection, enforcement) to additional configuration types beyond NGINX. Each new config type follows the same pattern but with config-specific adaptations.
work_items.target: Triggered by PA-OC-09 completion + leadership decision

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-11.1] Config Type Selection and Prioritization
work_items.target: Triggered by PA-OC-09 completion + leadership decision

work_items.summary: Review discovered config landscape (from PA-OC-02 discovery of broader config inventory)
Prioritize config types for pipeline pattern replication (candidates: PASOE configs, customer runtime configs, infrastructure configs)
Document selection rationale and sequencing
[PA-OC-11.2] Pipeline Framework Adaptation
work_items.target: Triggered by PA-OC-09 completion + leadership decision

work_items.summary: Adapt GitHub Actions workflows for new config type (build, deploy, drift detection logic)
Update runner strategy if needed for new config type
Update credential management approach for new config type if needed
Test full pipeline end-to-end in non-production
[PA-OC-11.3] Replication Playbook
work_items.target: Triggered by PA-OC-09 completion + leadership decision

work_items.summary: Document the pattern and procedures for replicating the pipeline framework to future config types
Include decision points and adaptation checklist
Include estimates and resource requirements for future replications
Serve as operational knowledge base for repeating this outcome for additional config types

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X1] Andy bottleneck: nearly all decisions require Andy, who may not have authority t...
action_items.description:
Risk: Andy bottleneck: nearly all decisions require Andy, who may not have authority to decide. Decisions escalate beyond him, adding weeks of delay.
Mitigation: Use delegation authority table; Type 2 decisions delegated to Brent, Rex, or Ryan as appropriate.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X2] SOC 2 compliance gap: Dynamo deliverables must meet SOC 2 even if current DMSi p...
action_items.description:
Risk: SOC 2 compliance gap: Dynamo deliverables must meet SOC 2 even if current DMSi practices do not. Retrofitting compliance after the fact is significantly more expensive.
Mitigation: Build SOC 2 requirements into every deliverable from PA-OC-01 forward.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X3] Secrets in config files: Certificates, IPs, and credentials are likely embedded ...
action_items.description:
Risk: Secrets in config files: Certificates, IPs, and credentials are likely embedded across config files on the share drive. Must be identified and managed before VCS migration.
Mitigation: Complete credential inventory in PA-OC-02.3; scan before any repository commit.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X4] Ansible automation infeasibility: If Ansible requires MFA or human interaction t...
action_items.description:
Risk: Ansible automation infeasibility: If Ansible requires MFA or human interaction to execute, deploy pipeline must be redesigned.
Mitigation: Validate in PA-OC-03.3; if infeasible, design alternative deploy mechanism.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X5] AWS account access: Runner infrastructure requires AWS account access that may t...
action_items.description:
Risk: AWS account access: Runner infrastructure requires AWS account access that may take weeks to provision.
Mitigation: Begin access request in PA-OC-01; escalate to Hilltop if unresolved.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X6] Scope creep to application deployment: DMSi may expect Caley's application deplo...
action_items.description:
Risk: Scope creep to application deployment: DMSi may expect Caley's application deployment work to be in scope.
Mitigation: Explicit scope documentation; escalate any scope expansion to Cameron.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X7] Shared database credentials embedded in config files create SOC 2 violations if ...
action_items.description:
Risk: Shared database credentials embedded in config files create SOC 2 violations if migrated to GitHub.
Mitigation: Complete PA-OC-02.3 credential management plan before any config migration.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-X8] Data classification gap: No formal data classification exists at DMSi today. Pip...
action_items.description:
Risk: Data classification gap: No formal data classification exists at DMSi today. Pipeline artifacts may contain sensitive data.
Mitigation: Complete PA-OC-01.4 early; define classification levels if none exist.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-01] Current deployment knowledge is concentrated in Rex. If Rex is unavailable durin...
action_items.description:
Risk: Current deployment knowledge is concentrated in Rex. If Rex is unavailable during this outcome, process documentation will be incomplete and downstream outcomes will be built on assumptions.
Mitigation: Schedule Rex walkthrough in week 1; document process in real-time
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete

action_items.summary: [PA-R-03] NGINX config inventory may be incomplete if undocumented configs exist outside t...
action_items.description:
Risk: NGINX config inventory may be incomplete if undocumented configs exist outside the known share drive locations.
Mitigation: Scope narrowed to NGINX configs specifically. Validate NGINX-relevant configs against recent production incidents to identify gaps.
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

action_items.summary: [PA-R-S1] Database scripts use a shared service account with hardcoded credentials. This p...
action_items.description:
Risk: Database scripts use a shared service account with hardcoded credentials. This prevents audit trail attribution. If any script is compromised, all scripts' access is compromised.
Mitigation: Rotate shared credential immediately; plan individual service accounts before configs enter VCS
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

action_items.summary: [PA-R-S2] Config migration to GitHub risks committing hardcoded credentials to version con...
action_items.description:
Risk: Config migration to GitHub risks committing hardcoded credentials to version control. The shared service account amplifies blast radius.
Mitigation: Scan all configs for credentials before any repository commit; enable GitHub secret scanning
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete

action_items.summary: [PA-R-04] If Ansible execution requires human interaction (MFA, button-pushing), the deplo...
action_items.description:
Risk: If Ansible execution requires human interaction (MFA, button-pushing), the deploy pipeline cannot be fully automated. Rex currently runs Ansible locally; it is unknown whether this can be automated.
Mitigation: Validate in PA-OC-03.3 before committing to PA-OC-06 pipeline design
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

action_items.summary: [PA-R-05] Migration format decisions delayed beyond two weeks will block PA-OC-04 progress. I...
action_items.description:
Risk: Migration format decisions delayed beyond two weeks will block PA-OC-04 progress. If configs cannot be obtained off the internal network drive, this risk escalates to blocker.
Mitigation: Time-box format decisions; use YAML as default where no strong preference exists.
action_items.link_to: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

action_items.summary: [PA-R-06] Pipeline reliability below 90% will undermine team trust and lead to manual work...
action_items.description:
Risk: Pipeline reliability below 90% will undermine team trust and lead to manual workarounds. Bigger risk is partial adoption unless SSH key access is restricted to pipeline-only operations.
Mitigation: Start with non-production environments to build confidence; comprehensive error handling.

action_items.summary: [PA-R-07] Drift detection may produce false positives if live configs include dynamically ...
action_items.description:
Risk: Drift detection may produce false positives if live configs include dynamically generated content.
Mitigation: Build normalization rules; tune thresholds based on initial results

action_items.summary: [PA-R-08] Team adoption resistance -- engineers may find the pipeline workflow slower than...
action_items.description:
Risk: Team adoption resistance -- engineers may find the pipeline workflow slower than manual changes for urgent fixes.
Mitigation: Provide emergency manual change process with post-hoc reconciliation; demonstrate pipeline speed in training

action_items.summary: [PA-Q-11] What is the CAB (Change Advisory Board) process and lead time for infrastructure changes at DMSi?
action_items.description:
Question: What is the CAB (Change Advisory Board) process and lead time for infrastructure changes at DMSi?
Feeds: PA-D-13
Required by: PA-OC-04 entry
